svg.title: Oracle vs Parquet on disk: two rows of order data stored in each format
svg.desc: Oracle stores complete rows sequentially. Parquet stores a footer index first, then column chunks with key+values together, using rep levels to mark row boundaries.
svg.text: read all columns even for SUM(price)

g.text: schema + byte offsets for each column chunk

svg.text: r=0 new row starts here (like \n in CSV)
r=1 next item in same row (like , in CSV)
